settings.title: Operations Dashboard for Lumen Logistics
settings.summary: We replaced a wall of spreadsheets with a live dashboard that shows fleet status at a glance.
settings.description:
Lumen's dispatch team tracked 200 vehicles across a dozen shared spreadsheets that were always slightly out of date. A driver could be stuck on the side of a highway for an hour before anyone at the desk knew, because the sheet that would have shown it had not been refreshed since morning.

Where they started

The spreadsheets were not the real problem; the lag was. Numbers came from two separate systems, the telematics platform and the order system, and a person had to copy between them. By the time the picture was assembled it described the past, not the present.

What we did

We built a single dashboard that pulls directly from both the telematics and order systems, so there is no manual copying and nothing to fall behind. The screen shows a live map of the fleet, flags delays as they happen, and lets managers export a clean daily report for the morning review.

  • A live fleet map fed by the telematics system
  • Delay alerts that surface a stalled route in real time
  • One-click daily reports for managers
  • A direct link to the order system, with no re-keying

A real challenge was the telematics feed, which dropped signal in low-coverage areas and would have made the map flicker. We added a short buffer that holds the last known position and marks it as stale rather than blanking it, so dispatchers always see something honest instead of a vehicle vanishing.

The result

Dispatchers now spot a stalled route in seconds instead of finding out at the end of the shift, and on-time delivery rose four points in three months. The dozen spreadsheets were retired, and the morning report that used to take an hour to assemble is now a single export.
